CodexDryChecker.docs: D3: pub surface (new, DryCheckAgentPort impl) is identical to baseline. The shared subprocess helpers spawn_codex, drain_pipe, runtime_path (unified with a base_dir parameter, replacing prepare_runtime_path), and codex_bin are pub(crate) in codex_common; tee_stderr_to_file is pub (see the functions catalogue entry for infrastructure::codex_common::tee_stderr_to_file). run_codex_child and AutoCleanup remain per-module in codex_dry_checker — run_codex_child is NOT shared (CN-03: its return type, output parsing, and kill strategy differ from the reviewer's).

CodexReviewer.docs: D3: pub surface (new, with_scope_label, Reviewer impl) is identical to baseline. The shared subprocess helpers spawn_codex, drain_pipe, runtime_path (unified with a base_dir parameter), and codex_bin are pub(crate) in codex_common; tee_stderr_to_file is pub (see the functions catalogue entry for infrastructure::codex_common::tee_stderr_to_file). run_codex_child and AutoManagedArtifacts remain per-module in codex_reviewer — run_codex_child is NOT shared (CN-03: its return type, output parsing, and kill strategy differ from the dry-checker's).

infrastructure::codex_common::tee_stderr.docs: Canonical stderr-tee helper extracted under ADR D3. Reads lines from a Codex subprocess ChildStderr, writes each line to the provided File, and echoes it to the process stderr. Now pub so the apps/cli planner can reuse it via cli_composition::tee_stderr_to_file (pub use infrastructure::codex_common::tee_stderr_to_file), eliminating the byte-identical copy in apps/cli/src/commands/plan/codex_local.rs. The other shared helpers (spawn_codex, drain_pipe, runtime_path, codex_bin) remain pub(crate); run_codex_child stays per-module in each adapter and is NOT shared (CN-03). CN-02 keeps the I/O helper in infrastructure (not usecase) where std::fs and std::process I/O is permitted.

infrastructure::dry_check::corpus::sha25.docs: D3: canonical SHA-256 -> lowercase hex encoder already in infrastructure::dry_check::corpus. The inline format!('{hash_bytes:x}') in codex_dry_checker.rs delegates to this function post-remediation, eliminating the semantic dup with corpus fingerprinting.
